pkg/pagination/v2.purpose: Generic cursor-based pagination library providing Cursor (time+ID composite, base64-encoded), Result[T], Paginator[T] interface, and CollectAll. All domain list queries needing stable keyset pagination must use these types — never offset-based pagination.

patterns.name: Item interface for cursor derivation
patterns.description: Any type passed to NewResult[T] must implement Item (Cursor() Cursor); NewResult derives the next-page cursor from the last element's Cursor() automatically.
patterns.example: func (e MyEntity) Cursor() pagination.Cursor { return pagination.NewCursor(e.CreatedAt, e.ID) }

patterns.name: Cursor encoding: base64(RFC3339,ID)
patterns.description: Cursors are opaque. Internal format is base64('<RFC3339 UTC>,<ID>'). Always use NewCursor/Encode/DecodeCursor; time is normalised to UTC; SplitN(limit=2) allows commas in IDs.
patterns.example: c := pagination.NewCursor(t, id); encoded := c.Encode(); decoded, err := pagination.DecodeCursor(encoded)

patterns.name: Paginator[T] via NewPaginator
patterns.description: Wrap any page-fetch function as a Paginator[T]; the function receives a *Cursor (nil on first call) and returns Result[T] with Items and NextCursor.

patterns.name: CollectAll with MAX_SAFE_ITER cap
patterns.description: CollectAll iterates until NextCursor is nil, capped at MAX_SAFE_ITER (10_000). Returns partial results alongside any mid-iteration error.

patterns.name: nil NextCursor signals last page
patterns.description: Result.NextCursor == nil means no further pages; NewResult sets it nil when the items slice is empty. Check before issuing another request.

key_file_guides.role: Item interface (Cursor() Cursor) and Paginator[T] interface with NewPaginator wrapping a closure.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding methods to Item breaks all implementors; keep it to Cursor() Cursor only.

key_file_guides.role: Cursor value type with base64 encode/decode, MarshalText/UnmarshalText, Validate, EncodePtr. Time is always UTC.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not change cursorDelimiter or encoding format — client-stored cursors break. SplitN(limit=2) intentionally allows commas in IDs.

key_file_guides.role: Result[T] and NewResult[T Item]; NextCursor derived from the last item's Cursor().
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewResult requires T to implement Item. When the caller controls the next cursor manually, build Result[T] directly without NewResult.

key_file_guides.role: CollectAll iterates a Paginator until exhausted or MAX_SAFE_ITER (10_000).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Silently stops at 10_000 without error; callers must handle both return values — partial results are returned alongside errors.

anti_patterns: Implementing offset-based pagination — this package is cursor-only.
Manually constructing the cursor string instead of NewCursor/Encode.
Calling NewResult[T] without T implementing Item.
Ignoring the (items, error) dual return from CollectAll — partial results are intentional.
Storing decoded Cursor fields externally and reconstructing — treat Cursor as opaque; re-encode via Encode().

decisions.decision: Cursor encodes both time and ID (composite key)
decisions.rationale: Time alone is not unique across rows; the ID disambiguates ties, enabling stable keyset pagination when records share a timestamp.
Prevents runaway memory if a Paginator never returns nil NextCursor; tests assert the cap is honoured.

decisions.decision: Paginator[T] as an interface wrapping a function via NewPaginator
decisions.rationale: Lets test paginators be closures without a named type, keeping test code concise.

code_examples.scenario: Domain list method returning paginated results with a next cursor
